title: YeeThereGate aggregator element: resolves when subtree settles
description: Implement <yee-there-gate> as a custom element that observes a subtree (default: subtree under its parent or itself) and exposes a ready() Promise that resolves when all in-flight yee-there holds on that subtree have cleared. Re-arms when a new yee-there event fires. Composes with DOM mutations — newly-added elements that emit yee-there re-open the gate. Used by yee-mix to gate applier processing on element readiness. Attribute: observe= (CSS selector or 'parent' / 'self').

title: YeeThereEvent class extends ExtendableEvent
description: Implement the core YeeThereEvent class as a subclass of ExtendableEvent. Dispatched by elements to register in-flight holds; waitUntil(promise) extends the hold. Must work standalone (no yee dep) — yee-there is a contract package. Includes: YeeThereEvent constructor, EventInit shape, the 'yee-there' static type constant. Tests: dispatching the event without waitUntil is a no-op hold; dispatching with waitUntil registers the hold; multiple waitUntil calls accumulate.

title: yee integration: gate mix processing on yee-there
description: yee-mix in v2 should gate its applier processing on yee-there events from its targets and from its applier children. An applier with async apply work emits yee-there; the mix does not consider the target 'fully applied' until the gate resolves. This gives yee the transactional apply semantics cast-yee and moqy need. Tracks on yee's side as a v2 ticket; this epic is the cross-link.
